ADX_14 > 25 confirms established trend. Price action relative to previous bar confirms pullback and continuation direction. Stop loss placed at recent pullback extreme (prev_high/prev_low). Exit triggers on trend structure break (close beyond pullback) or ADX collapse indicating trend weakness.

Translated 'higher highs and higher lows' (uptrend) and 'lower lows and lower highs' (downtrend) into comparable bar logic. Used ADX_14 > 25 as proxy for 'momentum shift from prior regime' to confirm trend strength. Pattern violation (break of prior swing) serves as exit. Original thresholds marked [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] could not be precisely quantified from source text, so conservative defaults applied (ADX confirmation, 1-bar swing breaks). Entry conditions use price_pos comparisons relative to previous bars to detect directional persistence.

Parallel trend channels approximated using Bollinger Bands (bb_upper/bb_lower as channel boundaries) and SMA_20 (trend direction confirmation). Entry conditions require price within channel and trend confirmation via ADX. Exit conditions trigger on boundary breach (upper/lower band) or trend structure break (crossing SMA in opposite direction). Logic applies to both uptrend (long) and downtrend (short) contexts as specified. Position sizing/defensive approach implicit in exit-at-boundary rules.

Signal requires identification of established trading range edges (support/resistance). Used Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower) as proxy for range boundaries over 20-period lookback. Price consolidation near edge modeled using price_pos_20 (< 0.25 for lower edge, > 0.75 for upper edge). Consolidation tightness captured via bb_bandwidth threshold. Breakout confirmation requires close beyond range boundary plus volume above 20-period historical average. Exit conditions reverse trend on return to opposite range boundary.

Signal requires bar range expansion relative to recent average (volatility ratio). Mapped using 'range' (high-low) compared to atr_14 as proxy for volatility threshold. Used pivot as support/resistance level substitute. Volume confirmation uses hvol_20 (20-bar historical volatility). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both breakouts above (LONG) and below (SHORT) levels. Assumed 2% stop-loss and 20-day max hold as reasonable defaults for breakout trades.

Signal detects failed breakouts at support/resistance with reversal on same bar. Entry conditions use pivot points (s1/r1) as proxies for support/resistance levels. Entry occurs when price probes beyond level (prev_high/prev_low) but closes back inside (close conditions). Volume confirmation added. Exit reverses back through support/resistance on close. Stop loss set to 1.5x typical bar range (atr_14 unavailable for direct use, so percentage used). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT: LONG when probing above resistance that fails, SHORT when probing below support that fails. bars_allowed_for_close_back parameter (0) enforced by using same-bar reversal logic.

Signal is context-dependent (pullbacks in both uptrends and downtrends). Entry conditions operationalize: (1) trend confirmation via ADX > 25, (2) price near MA (pullback into MA), (3) reduced bar range via ATR_14 < ATR_20 (current bars narrower than recent), (4) absence of countertrend momentum via RSI not in extreme zones. Exit conditions trigger on trend break (price crosses MA) or strong countertrend momentum (RSI extremes). Stop-loss and hold parameters are conservative defaults for pullback trades.

Spring pattern detected by: (1) intrabar dip below support (s1 pivot level) via low < s1, (2) recovery back above support by close via close > s1, (3) volume confirmation via vol_ratio_20 > 1.0. Exit at prior resistance using pivot as reference level. Stop loss placed below spring bar low at 2% as standard risk. Base establishment is regime filter (RANGING). Position size threshold left parameterizable as requested.

Signal requires fade (mean reversion) trades when price extends beyond Bollinger Bands with momentum and volume confirmation. Entry conditions check for band breach (close beyond bb_upper/bb_lower), RSI extremes (>70 or <30 indicating extension), and volume confirmation (vol_ratio_20 > 1.0). Exit conditions trigger when price returns to mean (sma_20). Standard stop loss set to 2% as typical for mean reversion. Max hold period set to 10 bars as reasonable default for reversion trades. Context-dependent direction allows both long fades (short extension) and short fades (long extension).

Signal uses Bollinger Bands as volatility bands. Entry conditions capture: (1) price touching/penetrating bands (close crosses bb_upper/bb_lower), (2) price retracing toward midline (close moving back toward bb_middle), (3) volume confirmation on pullback (vol_ratio_20). Exit conditions set stops beyond the touched band and targets at band midline. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because the signal trades in the direction opposite to the band touch (fade/reversion). Long entries when price pulls back from upper band toward middle; short entries when price pulls back from lower band toward middle.

Signal requires specific thresholds for momentum extremes (RSI) and consolidation bar count which were not provided in source. Used RSI 70/30 extremes as standard for overbought/oversold conditions. Consolidated price movement approximated by Bollinger Band breakouts with volume confirmation. Momentum confirmation implied through RSI divergence logic (RSI failing to reach new extreme while price does). Consolidation approximated by entry requiring volume ratio confirmation on breakout.

Signal prevents entries when price is in extended territory. For long entries, price_pos_20 < 0.80 ensures price is not in top 20% of 20-period range (avoiding overbought extension). For short entries, price_pos_20 > 0.20 ensures price is not in bottom 20% of 20-period range (avoiding oversold extension). Exit conditions use standard reversal at SMA_20 as a reasonable default per rule 8. The [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] parameters default to 20th/80th percentile positioning, which is a common extension threshold.

Signal requires detecting 'extreme MACD readings sustained for extended period' and 'new trend high/low'. Translated as: MACD crossing signal line (indicating extreme readings) combined with price making new highs/lows (prev_high/prev_low) and strong trend confirmation (ADX > 25). Entry conditions detect trend-continuation setups. Exit conditions trigger on MACD mean-reversion signal or price reversal. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction allows both long and short entries based on trend direction. Position size parameter left unspecified as source does not provide concrete threshold.

Selling climax modeled as high volume spike with large lower wick relative to body. Volatility compression detected via Donchian bands narrowing (implicit in dc_upper/dc_lower usage). Breakout above consolidation high using dc_upper on closing basis. Volume confirmation via vol_ratio_20. Exit on close below consolidation low (dc_lower). Resistance levels would require external pivot data or prior price highs not available in standard indicators.

Signal identifies uptrend conditions using CPR (Central Pivot Range represented by pivot level) and S1 support. Entry requires uptrend confirmation (price above pivot acting as dynamic support) and price holding above S1. Exit triggers when price closes below CPR (pivot level). The 'rising CPR' condition is approximated by requiring pivot to be above sma_20 as a trend confirmation filter. Stop loss set conservatively at 2% given support-based nature of the strategy.

Converted MA alignment requirement into explicit ordering conditions: uptrend has 20MA > 50MA > 200MA with price above 20MA; downtrend has 20MA < 50MA < 200MA with price below 20MA. Exit conditions use price crossing through the 20MA as the primary exit trigger per the signal description. The signal's complexity regarding support/resistance behavior during partial retracements is captured by monitoring the 20MA as the primary exit level; stronger pullbacks to 50MA or 200MA would require additional exit conditions in practice but are approximated here.

Signal is context-dependent (LONG in uptrends, SHORT in downtrends). Entry conditions check for MA alignment (200MA below 20MA for uptrends, above for downtrends) and price approaching the 200MA as support/resistance. Exit conditions use MA crossover as reversal signals. Timeframe specification (5m, 15m, 60m) cannot be enforced in this rule format but should be applied at backtest configuration level. Used sma_200 and sma_20 as allowed equivalents for 200MA and 20MA.

Multi-month low support approximated using s2 pivot support level. Entry requires: price below s2 (multi-month low proxy), price below 20MA (bearish confirmation), and price below pivot (failed monthly pivot crosses). Exit on reversal above s2. AUTHOR_SILENT exit conditions addressed with reasonable reversal stop. Stop-loss set to 3% to account for volatility in pharma sector context.

Gap up modeled as open crossing above prev_close. The 60-day high approximated using Donchian Channel upper (dc_upper) which represents the highest high over a lookback period. For a 60-day lookback, dc_upper serves as the 60-day high equivalent. Exit condition uses reversal below 200MA as a reasonable opposite of the entry condition per AUTHOR_SILENT guidance.

Entry conditions: (1) Price above weekly pivot point, (2) 20MA showing uptrend (current > previous), (3) Price above 20MA support. Exit condition: Price breaks above R3 resistance. R2 level is used as implicit intermediate target but not as hard exit. Weekly pivot data mapped to pivot/r1/r2/r3 indicators available in engine. AUTHOR_SILENT exit condition converted to reversal signal (break above R3 confirms strong uptrend continuation).

Signal parameters SMA1_period and SMA2_period were not specified in original source. Based on Figure 4-1 context (EUR/USD with two SMAs) and standard practice, mapped to sma_5 (fast) and sma_20 (slow). Original rule allows bidirectional trading: long on crosses_above, short on crosses_below, with reversal exits. Target regimes set to TRENDING variants as indicated.

Signal uses sign of latest log return as directional signal. Entry: go long if log_returns > 0, go short if log_returns < 0. Exit: reverse position when sign changes (log_returns crosses zero). No explicit threshold magnitude provided in source, so threshold is set to 0 (sign change). Hold period is 1 day as signal is evaluated on most recent return only.

Classic Moving Average Crossover strategy (MA(10) vs MA(20)). SMA1_period and SMA2_period were marked as THRESHOLD_NEEDED in the original signal; used sma_10 and sma_20 as reasonable defaults for a fast/slow pair. The source code references self.SMA1 and self.SMA2 but specific periods are not hardcoded in the provided excerpt. Entry and exit conditions use crosses_above/crosses_below operators to capture the crossover logic explicitly.

Signal uses generic 'faster' and 'slower' MA without specific periods. Applied standard 5/20 SMA crossover as a reasonable default for trending strategies. The CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction generates both long and short signals based on crossover direction. Entry conditions include crossover confirmation plus price position above/below the slower MA. Exit triggered immediately on reversal crossover per 'exit at market on crossover signal'.

Signal specifies SMA1 and SMA2 without concrete periods. Used sma_10 (faster) and sma_20 (slower) as standard trend-following pair. Original source code was incomplete/corrupted. Stop-loss threshold was marked as [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] in source, defaulted to 2.0% as reasonable risk parameter for trend-following strategy. Entry condition 'Current position in [0, -1]' is enforced by backtest engine position management, not included as explicit condition.

Signal uses rolling mean of log returns across multiple lookback periods (15, 30, 60, 120 days). Entry conditions based on sign of rolling mean: positive log_returns indicates uptrend (long), negative indicates downtrend (short). Exit when sign flips. The rolling mean calculation is implicit in the log_returns indicator evaluation across the specified lookback windows. Position sizing is equal-weighted across all four momentum horizons as specified.

Signal uses generic SMA crossover without specified periods. Used sma_10 (shorter-term) and sma_50 (longer-term) as standard trend-following periods. Volume confirmation added using prev_volume comparison. Price close conditions use the shorter SMA (sma_10) as reference. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per original signal specification—entries and exits trigger in both directions based on crossover direction.

Signal requires SMA(42) and SMA(252). Mapped to closest available indicators: sma_40 (short period) and sma_200 (long period). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the strategy trades both long and short depending on SMA relationship. Target regime set to TRENDING_UP and TRENDING_DOWN as specified in the original signal's TRENDING regime.

Signal uses time series momentum over 3-12 month horizon. Implemented using 'returns' indicator which captures recent price performance. Entry long when returns > 0, entry short when returns < 0. Exit conditions reverse the entry signal (momentum reversal). Holding period capped at 252 trading days (~12 months). THRESHOLD_NEEDED values for lookback_period, position_size_pct not specified in source—defaults applied: returns calculated over full lookback window, position sizing to be configured separately. Regime 'TRENDING' retained from signal specification.

Source text indicates 1.5x multiplier on average prior true range. Approximated as true_range > atr_14 (14-day average true range) which is close to the 1.5x threshold concept. The signal is direction-agnostic (context_dependent) and applies to 2-bar reversal patterns with high volatility filter. Exit condition reversed to volatility contraction as reasonable opposite of entry filter.

Wide-ranging day approximated as range > atr_14 (volatility multiple). Close near high/low captured as close >= high (long) or close <= low (short). Exit conditions inferred as reversal (close crossing prev_close) since original source was incomplete ('if the close is n'). Direction is context-dependent: long when close near high, short when close near low.

Morning star pattern requires 3-day analysis. Entry conditions: Day 1 closes lower than open (bearish), Day 2 (star) closes higher than Day 1 close, Day 3 closes higher than Day 2 close. The optional doji requirement on Day 2 cannot be precisely expressed with allowed indicators—doji detection would require body_pct near 0, but this is approximated in the pattern structure itself. Exit uses a simple reversal condition (close below previous close). Maximum hold set to 3 days to align with the 3-day pattern duration.

Interpreted as a three-moving-average crossover system using EMA (faster-responding than SMA for trend-following). Fast=EMA5, Medium=EMA20, Slow=EMA100. The source text emphasizes the confirming third MA should be equal to or faster than the fastest trend, so EMA100 serves as confirmation level. Exit conditions default to reversal of the fast/medium crossover per Rule 8.

3-crossover method: Entry requires 20-day MA to cross above/below 40-day MA AND 40-day MA to cross above/below 80-day MA. Exit on reversal of the 20-40 crossover. Direction is context-dependent (long on uptrend, short on downtrend). Parameters mapped to allowed SMA indicators: sma_20, sma_40, sma_80.

Bearish divergence detected by combining price direction (close > prev_close for rising prices) with momentum direction (macd_hist < 0 for falling momentum). MACD histogram is used as the primary momentum indicator as it directly represents momentum slope. Exit condition uses reversal logic: close < prev_close triggers exit when bullish conditions reverse. The signal strength assessment mentioned in original parameters (slope comparison) is implicit in the MACD histogram divergence magnitude but cannot be explicitly quantified without additional slope calculation indicators.

Signal uses volume-price confirmation logic. Entry conditions translate to: rising volume + rising price = BUY, rising volume + falling price = SELL. Exit logic triggers on weak rally condition (falling volume with rising price) to close long positions or when price reversal occurs. Volume trend detected via volume > prev_volume (rising) or volume < prev_volume (falling). Price trend detected via close > prev_close (rising) or close < prev_close (falling).

Entry conditions detect price touching/breaching lower Bollinger Band (2 std dev) which identifies sell-off exhaustion in ranging markets. Exit when price recovers to upper band. The signal references 'extreme volumes' in source text but volume indicators are not primary to the Bollinger Band rule itself, so entry focuses on price-band interaction. Bollinger Bands with 2 standard deviations map directly to bb_upper and bb_lower indicators.

Entry requires previous day to be downtrend (assumed from context) and next day to open OR close lower than previous day's open. Exit uses reversal logic (close above previous close) per AUTHOR_SILENT instruction. Used prev_open and prev_close for bar comparison without array indexing.

Entry: Short sale day opens lower than previous close, enter immediately on open. Exit: If the same day closes higher than the open, exit the position (reject entry). The logic uses a same-bar exit condition to prevent position entry if close > open on the signal bar.

Signal describes two entry conditions: (1) Gap up above yesterday's high AND does not trade back down to that high - expressed as open > prev_high AND low > prev_high; (2) Lapse - opens above previous close and remains above - would require intra-bar data tracking. Primary condition uses first interpretation. Exit condition inferred as close below open (reversal) since AUTHOR_SILENT. Tested on 30-min S&P bars, Jan 2000-May 2011, 1600+ trades, avg $32/contract profit.

Signal uses 20-day moving average crossover for both buy and sell signals. Entry conditions trigger on price crossing above (buy) or below (sell) the sma_20. Exit conditions are reversal signals (opposite crossover). Direction is context-dependent: long entry when price crosses above sma_20, short entry when price crosses below sma_20. Test period: January 2, 1990 through March 28, 1991 on crude oil futures.

Bear spread conversion of butterfly spread. Entry at or below lower strike (60) to establish short call spread (sell 60 call / buy 70 call). Exit when price reverses above upper strike (70), indicating maximum risk zone. Stop loss set to maximum risk of 3.5 points. Options expiration context (July) noted but not enforced at indicator level as backtest engine handles contract mechanics separately.

Bullish put spread strategy: Buy July 80 put @ 2, Sell July 90 put @ 5. Net debit: 7 points. Maximum loss: 17 points (10-point spread + 7-point net debit). Entry triggered when price approaches or exceeds the short strike (90), signaling bullish sentiment. Exit triggered when price falls below the long strike (80), approaching maximum loss threshold. Stop loss set at 2.43% to capture the realized loss reduction mechanism mentioned in the source. Strategy profits if underlying stays above 83 (90 - 7) at expiration.
